screenshots.note: Buttons enumerated: ↺ sync, ☀️ theme, 🏢 Office, 📊 Dashboard, 📋 and ℹ️ nav buttons, ✕ Close × 3, filter buttons (All/Hires/Completions/Errors/System), worker filter buttons (All Workers, Luna, Carlos, Maya, Dave, Sofia), and footer tagline quote button.

summary: Review #99. Two solid fixes land: isTouchDevice correctly moved inside App() via useMemo (STRIKE-6 item, finally done), and vibe history push is now properly guarded with isInitialMount (STRIKE-6 item, done). Three delightful features shipped: STANDUP badge on the conference table, last-huddle timer tracking, and a vibe-transition caption overlay with 90s cooldown — all charming and well-built. The thought bubble quips grew from 4 to 20+ vibe-reactive strings, dramatically increasing replayability. However: 4 of the 6 STRIKE-6 items remain broken and are now STRIKE-7. Worker cards tabIndex=-1 is eight characters of change. ErrorBoundary #f87171 is two lines. Mobile hover card dismiss is five lines of JSX. App.jsx grew from 1482 to 1590 lines (+108) and Office.jsx from 1823 to 1863 lines (+40) — the monolith is still growing. A new ref-as-prop bug in lastHuddle causes stale huddle data in ConferenceTable. The double RAF handles in the thought bubble useEffect remain uncancelled on unmount (carried from #98).

suggestions.title: Worker cards tabIndex={-1} — STRIKE 7, truly blocking
suggestions.description: WorkerCard outer div at App.jsx line 396 is permanently tabIndex={-1}. Screen-reader and keyboard users cannot navigate to any worker card. Seven consecutive reviews. The fix is eight characters: change tabIndex={-1} to tabIndex={0}. The inner elements correctly manage their own tabIndex (lines 398, 411, 433, 457, 462). This is a WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 and SC 2.4.3 failure. At this point the card is not keyboard-focusable at any state and the aria-label on the outer div (which reads name/role/status) is unreachable by keyboard users. Unacceptable for seven cycles.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary fallback hardcoded dark-mode color — STRIKE 7
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 17: color '#f87171' is hardcoded in the ErrorBoundary inline style. On a light-mode browser the red-400 text is invisible against white. Seven reviews, zero action. Fix is two lines: either color: 'var(--text-error, #f87171)' or extract to App.css .error-boundary-msg { color: #f87171 } and add a light-mode override. The button next to it (line 18) also has no color specified and will inherit the parent red, making the button text illegible in light mode too.

suggestions.title: Mobile pinned hover card has no visible dismiss control — STRIKE 7
suggestions.description: On touch devices, tapping a character calls setPinnedCard (Office.jsx line 844) which pins the hover card open. Dismissal requires tapping outside the .char container (Office.jsx line 780: if (!e.target.closest('.char')) setPinnedCard(false)). This interaction is completely undiscoverable. Seven consecutive reviews with no fix. The hover card renders at Office.jsx line 931 inside the char-hover-card div. Add a close button: {pinnedCard && <button className='char-hover-card__close' onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); setPinnedCard(false) }} aria-label='Close worker card'>✕</button>}. Five lines of JSX plus two CSS lines.

suggestions.title: App.jsx 1590 lines, Office.jsx 1863 lines — monolith keeps growing — STRIKE 7
suggestions.description: App.jsx grew from 1482 → 1590 lines (+108 this cycle). Office.jsx grew from 1823 → 1863 lines (+40). Combined source: 3453 lines (was 3305, +148). Seven cycles, zero splitting. STANDUP logic (lines 1331-1332 in Office.jsx), ConferenceTable (line 1015, 170 lines of component), AwaySign (line 584), thought bubble logic (lines 1348-1463), and VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS/WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES/LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS are all splitworthy. Proposed plan: ConferenceTable.jsx (own file), AwaySign.jsx (own file), officeConstants.js (VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS, WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES, LONE_SURVIVOR_*). That alone removes ~400 lines from Office.jsx.

suggestions.title: NEW: lastHuddle ref passed as prop — stale data in ConferenceTable
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1699 passes lastHuddle (a useRef) to ConferenceTable as a prop. React does not re-render on ref mutation — ConferenceTable will never update when lastHuddle.current changes unless its parent re-renders for another reason. The 'Last: [names] · [time]' display (lines 1113-1120 inside ConferenceTable) reads lastHuddle.current at render time, but since the ref mutation at lines 1396-1402 does not trigger a re-render, the display can show stale or missing huddle data. Fix: lift lastHuddle from a ref to useState so mutations cause re-renders, or pass lastHuddle.current (the plain object) as the prop and update it via setState.

suggestions.title: Speech bubble escapes office floor container on mobile — STRIKE 6
suggestions.description: Characters near the top of the office floor render their speech bubble (bottom: calc(100% + 8px)) outside the clipped container. On 375px width the bubble clips or disappears. The parent .office-floor uses overflow: clip with overflow-clip-margin: 20px (Office.css lines 84-85) but top-row characters at top: ~4% still overflow. Fix: detect top-row characters and flip bubble direction downward, or add a CSS clamp on the bubble's bottom position relative to the floor.

suggestions.title: Thought bubble double RAF handles not cancelled on unmount — STRIKE 2
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1447-1448: two nested requestAnimationFrame calls inside showBubble() are not tracked or cancelled in the useEffect cleanup. The cleanup at line 1458 correctly cancels thoughtScheduleRef and thoughtHideRef (the setTimeout handles), but if Office unmounts between the two RAFs, the second RAF fires setThoughtBubble on a component that is no longer mounted. React 18 is tolerant but the handles should still be cancelled for correctness. Fix: const raf1 = requestAnimationFrame(() => { const raf2 = requestAnimationFrame(...); rafRef2.current = raf2 }); rafRef1.current = raf1; then cancel both in cleanup.

suggestions.title: vibeCaption aria-live region is conditionally rendered — misses announcements
suggestions.description:
Office.jsx line 1701: {vibeCaption &&
{vibeCaption}
}. The live region is created and destroyed with the content. Screen readers register aria-live regions at page load; a region that appears and disappears dynamically may not be picked up by all assistive technology. The correct pattern is to always render the live region but empty it when inactive:
{vibeCaption ?? ''}
. Same pattern applies to the full-sync and full-idle banners at lines 1527-1528 (those are always rendered when active, so they also benefit from this change).

suggestions.title: Footer quote button missing aria-label — STRIKE 5
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1581: the footer-tagline button has no aria-label. The accessible name becomes the full rendered quote text (e.g., '✦"if it compiles, ship it."'), which is the quote content — not the button's purpose. Screen readers read the full quote on focus, which is disorienting and not actionable. Add aria-label='Cycle team quote' to communicate the button's purpose. Five cycles, trivial fix.

suggestions.title: Character tooltips in CSS ::after — not in accessibility tree — STRIKE 5
suggestions.description: Character name/status tooltips rendered via CSS content attr(data-tooltip) are invisible to screen readers. The hover card (char-hover-card) is more informative but only appears on hover/click, not on keyboard focus. Keyboard users navigating the office floor get no task or status information from characters. Fix: add aria-describedby on the character .char element pointing to a visually-hidden span containing tooltip text, or ensure keyboard focus triggers the hover card the same way mouse hover does.

suggestions.title: footer-tagline button uses key prop to force DOM remount — anti-pattern — STRIKE 4
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1582: key={`${teamVibe.key}-${quoteBonus}`} on the footer button causes React to unmount and remount the button on every vibe change or quote click. This destroys focus (keyboard users lose their position), resets CSS transitions, and causes unnecessary DOM churn. Use a ref plus an imperative CSS animation class toggle instead of key remounting. The sparkle animation can be triggered by adding/removing a class via a ref rather than remounting the element.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar renders unconditionally — root cause in normalize() — STRIKE 4
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 452: correctly guards with `worker.progress !== null` before rendering ProgressBar — this is now properly fixed from a prior review. However, normalize() at line 930 sets progress: typeof w.progress === 'number' ? w.progress : null, so when the API omits the field, progress is null and ProgressBar is correctly skipped. Verify this works end-to-end — the aria-valuenow at line 322 still falls back to 0 when progress is non-numeric, so a malformed progress value (e.g. a string) would silently show 0%. Add Number.isFinite(w.progress) in normalize() instead of typeof check.

suggestions.title: standupActive computed inline — not memoized — minor perf issue
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1331: const standupActive = workingWorkers.length >= 3 && (vibe === 'crushing' || vibe === 'in-flow') is computed as a plain boolean in the render body. While this boolean is used in the standupWorkers useMemo on line 1332, it re-evaluates on every render. Wrap in useMemo(() => workingWorkers.length >= 3 && (vibe === 'crushing' || vibe === 'in-flow'), [workingWorkers.length, vibe]) to be consistent with the rest of the derived state pattern in this file.

suggestions.title: Office window flat rectangle in dark mode — STRIKE 3
suggestions.description: Office.css line 1055: .office-window uses border: 2px solid var(--border) which renders as a nearly-invisible flat square in dark mode. Add a 2-3px inset box-shadow in a slightly lighter tone and a thin top highlight to simulate a window sill. Minimal effort, meaningful polish.

suggestions.title: Stats bar may wrap to 2 rows at mid-range viewports — STRIKE 5
suggestions.description: App.css line 619: at 600-900px the stats bar shifts to repeat(3, 1fr). A 5-stat grid in 3 columns always produces a partial second row. Either hide secondary stats at this breakpoint or use repeat(auto-fit, minmax(120px, 1fr)) for fluid columns.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label uses inline style block instead of CSS class — STRIKE 2
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1772: the lone-survivor label uses a 9-property inline style object directly in JSX (fontSize, opacity, position, bottom, left, transform, whiteSpace, color). This is inconsistent with the rest of the file. Move to a .lone-survivor-label class in Office.css. Two cycles of this pattern.

suggestions.title: NEW: lastHuddle inline style block in ConferenceTable
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1113-1120: the 'last huddle' display inside ConferenceTable uses an inline style object with 4 properties (opacity, fontSize, textAlign, marginTop) directly in JSX. Move to a .conf-last-huddle CSS class in Office.css for consistency.

verified_fixes: isTouchDevice moved inside App() via useMemo — FIXED. App.jsx line 778 shows const isTouchDevice = useMemo(() => typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches, []) inside the App component body, with a clarifying comment above. This was a STRIKE-6 item. Clean fix.

Vibe history push guarded with isInitialMount — FIXED. App.jsx lines 1198-1204: if (!isInitialMount) { ... history.push({ key: teamVibe.key, ts: Date.now() }) ... setVibeHistory(history) }. The isInitialMount check (previousVibeKeyRef.current === null, line 1164) now correctly guards both the streak counter and the history array push. This was a STRIKE-6 item. Full fix.

Thought bubble quips expanded massively — from 4 static strings to 20+ vibe-reactive quips covering all vibe states, error counts, isFullSync, isFullIdle, isLoneSurvivor, and workingWorkers.length. The breadth is excellent: vibe-specific messages, error counts, lone survivor, full idle, full sync — all distinct. Review #98 complaint fully addressed.

STANDUP feature landed — when vibe is 'crushing' or 'in-flow' and ≥3 workers are active, standupWorkers array is populated (Office.jsx line 1331-1332) and the conference table displays a STANDUP badge with those workers rendered around the table (lines 1140-1155). Correctly mutually exclusive with the Huddle badge. Well-structured.

Huddle timer tracking landed — lastHuddle ref tracks startedAt/endedAt for meeting sessions. When a huddle ends and was within 30 minutes, 'Last: [names] · [time]' appears below the conference table (lines 1103-1120). getRelativeTime is correctly used for the timestamp. Note: ref-as-prop issue noted in suggestions above.

Vibe caption overlay landed — on vibe change, a 2.8-second overlay caption appears in the office floor with vibe-specific emoji+text (Office.jsx lines 1427-1438), with a 90s debounce (lastVibeCaptionRef) to avoid spamming on rapid vibe changes. Clean implementation.
nameplate-pop animation on vibe change — nameplateRef.current.classList.add('nameplate-pop') is triggered on vibe change with proper cleanup (line 1419). Adds tactile feedback to vibe transitions.
No console.log or console.warn calls in production source — still clean.
ProgressBar conditional rendering confirmed — App.jsx line 452: worker.progress !== null correctly guards ProgressBar rendering.
strike_five_hall_of_shame: Worker cards tabIndex={-1} — SEVEN consecutive reviews. App.jsx line 396. Eight characters to fix. This is now a WCAG failure in production for seven release cycles.
ErrorBoundary hardcoded #f87171 — SEVEN consecutive reviews. App.jsx line 17. Two lines of change. Light-mode users see invisible error text.
Mobile pinned hover card no dismiss control — SEVEN consecutive reviews. Office.jsx char-hover-card. Five lines of JSX. Users are trapped with no discoverable way to close.
App.jsx + Office.jsx monolithic files — SEVEN consecutive reviews. Combined lines GREW from 3305 to 3453 (+148). App.jsx is now 1590 lines, Office.jsx is now 1863 lines.
Footer tagline button missing aria-label — FIVE consecutive reviews. App.jsx line 1581. Trivial fix. Screen readers read the full quote instead of the button purpose.
Character tooltips in CSS ::after not in accessibility tree — FIVE consecutive reviews. Keyboard users cannot access character status information.
Speech bubble escapes office floor container on mobile — SIX consecutive reviews. Characters near top:4% overflow the clipped container on 375px width.
praise: The STANDUP feature is the best new feature in several reviews. standupActive (vibe 'crushing'/'in-flow' + ≥3 workers) triggering the conference table STANDUP badge with workers rendered as avatars around the table is both visually charming and semantically meaningful. The mutual exclusion with the Huddle path (standupWorkers.length === 0 guard at line 1116) is correct.

Thought bubble quips expanding from 4 to 20+ is exactly what was asked for in #98. The coverage is thorough: 2-error 'This is fine 🔥', 1-error 'rough day', full sync, lone survivor, isFullIdle, all 5 vibe states with multi-worker variants, and a sensible fallback. This is genuinely fun to read.

The vibe caption overlay with 90-second debounce is elegant UX. Rapid vibe changes will not spam the caption, and the 2.8-second display time is just right. lastVibeCaptionRef.current = Date.now() as a debounce signal is the correct pattern (no state update triggered, no re-render, just a timestamp check).

isTouchDevice fix is clean and the comment above it ('Evaluated inside the component (via useMemo) to stay fresh on hybrid devices') is the first genuinely useful inline comment in this file in several reviews.

isInitialMount guard on the vibe history push is correct and symmetric with the streak guard above it. The previousVibeKeyRef.current === null sentinel is still the right pattern. Both guards now live together.

The huddle duration display ('Huddle · 3m') using Date.now() - startedAt inline at line 1123-1129 with tabular-nums font variant is a tiny but polished touch. The <1m case returning null instead of '0m' is the correct UX decision.
